Description:
Crowding - ln(POPDEN×POPPCT) 
This formula is a proxy for contact intensity, it captures how densely people are concentrated and how much of the population is exposed to those conditions. Multiplying the variables captures both how crowded environments are and how many people experience them, while the logarithm reduces the impact of extreme values and reflects that increases in density matter less at higher levels. Higher values indicate that a larger portion of the population is living in dense environments with greater potential for frequent contact and disease transmission, whereas lower values reflect more dispersed populations with fewer opportunities for spread.
